stemHtml: How does infrared satellite imagery compare regarding day/night usability and capability to differentiate between high-altitude and low-altitude clouds?

options.a: It can only be used at night and clearly distinguishes between high- and low-level clouds.
options.b: It can only be used during the day but gives an accurate indication of cloud height.
options.c: It can be used both day and night, but it does not reliably distinguish between high-and low-altitude clouds.
options.d: It can only be used for low-level clouds and requires visible light.
